name: Windstream Enterprise (Kinetic) API
what_it_does: Windstream Enterprise (now Kinetic Business) REST API for enterprise networking, SD-WAN, and managed services. Enables AI agents to manage SD-WAN network configuration and policy management for enterprise branch networking, handle unified communications and hosted voice service management for enterprise telephony, access broadband and dedicated internet service management for enterprise connectivity, retrieve network performance monitoring and SLA tracking for enterprise network operations, manage managed security service configuration for enterprise cybersecurity, handle service order management and circuit provisioning for network procurement, access customer portal and self-service management for enterprise account operations, retrieve invoice and billing data for connectivity cost management, manage managed Wi-Fi and network access service configuration, and integrate Windstream network data with ITSM, SD-WAN, and enterprise IT management platforms.
use_cases: Managing SD-WAN from enterprise network automation agents
Monitoring service performance from network operations agents
Configuring unified communications from enterprise voice agents
Managing broadband from connectivity management agents
not_for: Large enterprise requiring national carrier backbone services
Consumer broadband without enterprise managed services focus
Urban-only enterprises without Windstream's rural enterprise coverage
best_when: A mid-market or distributed enterprise with rural and suburban locations using Windstream wants AI agents to automate SD-WAN management, network monitoring, and unified communications.
avoid_when: NETWORK AVAILABILITY RISK: Automated SD-WAN and network service changes without change management create production network outage risk. Windstream emerged from Chapter 11 bankruptcy (2019-2020) and rebranded consumer division as Kinetic; verify current enterprise API support and service availability. Windstream's geographic footprint is primarily rural/suburban US — not appropriate for dense urban enterprise networking.

auth.notes: Windstream uses OAuth 2.0 and API key. No native webhooks. REST API with JSON. Little Rock, Arkansas HQ. Private (post-Chapter 11 emergence 2020). Founded 1883 (predecessor companies). Windstream Enterprise serves mid-market businesses. Consumer division rebranded Kinetic by Windstream. Competes with Lumen, Cox Business, and Spectrum Enterprise for mid-market enterprise networking.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — contract-based enterprise pricing
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual contract; bundled managed services pricing
pricing.notes: Little Rock, Arkansas. Windstream private (Chapter 11 emergence 2020). Mid-market enterprise and rural focus. Competes with Lumen and Cox Business for mid-market enterprise managed services.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; documented per contract

known_agent_gotchas: NETWORK AVAILABILITY RISK: Automated SD-WAN and network changes without change management create production outage risk for enterprise branch locations
Chapter 11 bankruptcy legacy — Windstream emerged from bankruptcy 2020; business stability and API investment uncertain; verify current developer program support
No webhooks — network event monitoring requires polling; no native event notification for service degradation or provisioning
Rural footprint limitation — Windstream primarily serves rural and suburban US markets; enterprise deployments in major metros may have limited service availability
Limited API documentation — Windstream developer resources less comprehensive than larger carriers; integration may require Windstream professional services
Consumer/Enterprise brand separation — Windstream Enterprise and Kinetic (consumer) are separate divisions; verify enterprise API vs. consumer portal access
agent_readiness.security_notes: Enterprise managed services networking. SOC2. OAuth2/API key. US. Network service and connectivity data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Enterprise networking. SOC2. OAuth2. US. Network service data.
